goal: Close the ONE hole #1242 names: re-build+run the host-independent Go witness TestQwen35HybridViaMMMatchesCPUTemplate that the prior ultracode dogfood (2026-06-28) recorded as a policy-blocked SKIP, capture it as a gradeable witness at a witnessed commit, and have an independent multi-agent wave re-check it. The full Mac M3 Pro parity gate (tools/qwen36_mac_parity_gate.sh, Arms 1-3) is NOT run here: this orchestrator has no Apple Silicon, no 27B GGUF, and no llama.cpp b9707 — those arms stay host-blocked, recorded as SKIP, never as PASS.
orchestrator_host: windows/amd64 (MINGW64_NT-10.0-26200, x86_64) — no Apple Silicon, no NVIDIA GPU, no 27B artifact
honesty_note: Per docs/proofs/00-METHOD.md a SKIP is not a PASS. Exactly one arm below ran here (the host-independent build-hole witness); it is recorded as a real PASS with its build output. The three Apple-Silicon/Metal/27B-gated arms are recorded as SKIP with the host capability each needs and the recorded prior witness each cites; none is rounded up to a pass. No speed/GPU/27B figure was re-measured on this box.

build_hole_arm.dod_item: Build hole closed: the host-independent Go witness TestQwen35HybridViaMMMatchesCPUTemplate builds + passes green in a real build (not a policy-blocked SKIP).
build_hole_arm.prior_state: In the 2026-06-28 ultracode dogfood (experiments/agent-live/ultracode-dogfood-witness-20260628.json, wave_2 build-sanity lane) this build was POLICY_BLOCK/TERMINAL in the subagent sandbox -> recorded as SKIP, counted as 0 deliverables. #1242 closes that hole with a real build.

mac_gate_arms.note: These are the three arms of tools/qwen36_mac_parity_gate.sh. On this windows/amd64 box the gate's own precondition (uname -s == Darwin) fails, so the script would print 'SKIP: not macOS' and exit non-zero before any arm. Each arm is recorded here as SKIP with the host it needs; the recorded prior witness each cites is the figure of record, NOT re-measured.

verification_wave.mode: ultracode multi-agent (4 independent disjoint adversarial lanes, run via the Workflow orchestrator on this box; each instructed to REFUTE, not rubber-stamp; read-only, edits_made=none)
verification_wave.overall: 4/4 PASS — every claim corroborated with independent evidence; one actionable correction folded back (the scrub tool, see scrub.tool_mismatch_caught).

lanes.evidence: Independently re-ran: `go test ./internal/model -run TestQwen35HybridViaMMMatchesCPUTemplate -count=1 -v` -> '--- PASS (0.05s)' / 'ok ... internal/model 1.361s' exit 0; `go build ./internal/model` exit 0; `CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ...` 'ok' exit 0. Bash was NOT policy-blocked here, so the prior wave's hole is genuinely re-runnable. Test body carries real assertions (assertQuantLogitsClose/assertKVCacheQuantClose/assertLinearAttnCacheQuantClose), not a no-op.

(a) build_hole_arm.result=PASS reproduced; (b) all three mac_gate_arms status=SKIP, none rounded to a pass; (c) recorded_prior ids match the pinned sources EXACTLY (fak [248068,198,8160], llama [248068,198,90700], divergence index 2); (d) commit cf8af435 confirmed ancestor of origin/main (merge-base --is-ancestor exit 0); (e) all DoD fields present. lanes.actionable_catch: the issue-named tools/leak_scan.py is the WRONG tool for a secret scrub; the witness scrub block was corrected to cite check_secret_shapes.py as the substantive gate (see scrub below).

scrub.substantive_gate: tools/check_secret_shapes.py --audit-tree -> 'secret-shape: clean (tracked tree).' exit 0 (covers the tracked rollup edit)
scrub.untracked_witness_coverage: this witness JSON is still untracked, so --audit-tree does not cover it; it was scanned by direct shape greps (IPv4 / /Users / .local / ts.net / tailnet / ssh keys / api tokens -> 0 hits) and a recursive per-field JSON shape scan (NONE), both clean
scrub.tool_mismatch_caught: the issue body and this witness initially named tools/leak_scan.py as THE scrub gate; the verification wave found leak_scan.py is a Go goroutine/memory-leak candidate scanner (walks only .go files), so its exit-0 'clean' on a .json/.md is vacuous and proves nothing about secret leakage. leak_scan.py was still run (exit 0) but the real secret gate is check_secret_shapes.py, recorded above.
scrub.result: no real host/user/IP/key in any committed artifact
